observed_matches: D1 satisfied: every cited coordinate resolves. The enforcement asymmetry is real in code — the driver post-completion gates use only _path_forbidden_by_write_scope (hold) and _path_allowed_by_write_scope (fake-landing = `not any(allowed)`), so a mixed diff [one allowed path + one non-forbidden path outside allowed_paths] fires NEITHER gate (forbidden=False; `not any(allowed)`=False because an allowed path exists) and the outside path lands. The outside-scope observation exists only as a recorded comparison fact (comparison.py:91-94 / write_observation.py:145-146), never consulted by a driver hold — confirmed by 0 support/operator matches for the bucket name.

D2 disposition direction matches code: B-only (no allowed path) → `not any(allowed)`=True → fake_landing_write_scope_diff_absent (driver.py:962-977); A_plus_B → neither gate → passes. Prior Brick honestly corrected its own narrow B-only hypothesis (rejected) and isolated the actual pass path A_plus_B — correct lane behavior for an honest self-correcting return.

D3 satisfied: two repair candidates returned (driver post-completion predicate for any path outside allowed_paths independent of allowed; route observed_paths_outside_declared_scope into an explicit gate), each with a stated surface risk, made_changes=false — no implementation, matching the investigation-only termination line.
Hard constraints honored: no repo-source mutation, no project/ residue, no git commit; worktree HEAD/path-count unchanged.
Prior Brick honestly recorded the absent evidence root t1s2v4-bothgates-0704a under blocked_or_missing_evidence — independently confirmed absent by glob.
